event: total thyroidectomy for a multinodular goitre
no history of obstructive sleep apnoea
converted to emergency Roux-en-Y gastric bypass surgery
transfer to intensive care at a tertiary hospital
Endocrinology advice required throughout her admission
triiodothyronine up titrated to 40/20/40 µg daily
intravenous calcium gluconate 4.4 mmol five times daily
ionised calcium measured daily via routine blood gases
serum phosphate remained in the normal range
intravenous calcitriol 1 µg alternate daily commenced
still required intravenous calcium gluconate 4.4 mmol five times daily
intravenous calcitriol 1 µg alternate daily recommenced
intravenous thyroxine 200 µg alternate daily commenced
calcium gluconate requirements reduced to 4.4 mmol three times per day
maintenance schedule established controlling hypocalcaemia and hypothyroidism
calcium carbonate 1200 mg orally twice daily at discharge
